items.name: Sweetheart Dino Plush

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 2 pieces for the dinosaur body following the paper pattern. These pieces form the main front and back of the project.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 1 piece for the dinosaur top section following the paper pattern. This piece forms the upper decorative layer.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 1 piece for the dinosaur bottom section following the paper pattern. This piece completes the body structure.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 6 small heart pieces for the dinosaur decoration using the paper pattern. These will be attached as decorative details.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 5 decorative heart pieces following the paper pattern. These pieces will be used as embellishments.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:
Sew slowly around curves to keep the heart shape neat.
Do not sew the bottom opening so the piece can be turned later.
Place two heart pieces right sides facing and sew around the edges, leaving the bottom edge open. Repeat for all heart units.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Use a blunt tool to shape curves without damaging fabric.
Gently push out the curves for a clean heart shape.
Turn each heart piece right side out through the bottom opening and shape the edges

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Join sew
steps.content:
Pin along the seam to prevent shifting.
Match the center points before sewing to keep the shape balanced.
Align the dinosaur upper piece and lower piece, then sew along the connecting seam to join them together.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Seam sew
steps.content:
Sew slowly along curves for smoother results.
Keep edges aligned while sewing curved sections.
Sew the dinosaur body side pieces to the joined upper and lower body section to form the full body shape

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Insert sew
steps.content:
Clip curves before turning to reduce bulk.
Remember to leave a 5 cm opening for turning and stuffing.
Place the finished heart pieces inside the dinosaur body following the marked positions. Sew around the dinosaur body edge and leave a 5 cm turning opening on the back.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Use a blunt stick to shape edges.
Push out corners and curves gently for a smooth shape.
Turn the dinosaur body right side out through the turning opening

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Fill stuff
steps.content:
Add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly.
Do not overfill, or the seams may become strained.
Insert stuffing through the opening until the dinosaur body is evenly filled.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling—add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly to prevent seam strain.

steps.title: Hand close
steps.content:
Use ladder stitch for an invisible seam.
Keep stitches small and tight for a neat finish.
Close the turning opening with hand stitching to secure the stuffing inside.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Attach stitch
steps.content:
Use small stitches to keep the decoration secure.
Check placement before sewing permanently.
Hand stitch the decorative heart onto the dinosaur body at the desired position.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

items.name: Animal Bookmark

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance of 0.5 cm is already included
Cut 2 pieces for the upper head section following the paper pattern. These pieces form the top shape of the head.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance of 0.5 cm is already included
Cut 2 pieces for the lower head section following the paper pattern. These pieces complete the head
structure.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance of 0.5 cm is already included
Cut 2 pieces for the bookmark piece following the paper pattern. These will form the hanging bookmark section.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Align sew
steps.content:
Use pins to keep small pieces aligned.
Match the pattern numbers carefully before sewing to ensure correct assembly.
Align the upper head, lower head, and bookmark pieces according to the numbered pattern edges, then sew them together to form the front side.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Seam sew
steps.content:
Check alignment before stitching.
Make sure the seam lines match the front side for a balanced shape.
Repeat the same sewing process for the back side pieces following the same numbered edges.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:
Mark the turning opening before sewing.
Do not sew the 4 cm turning gap so the piece can be turned later.
Place the two assembled bookmark pieces right sides facing and sew around the edges, leaving a 4 cm turning opening.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.content:
Use a blunt tool to shape corners.
Gently push out edges and curves to shape the piece neatly.
Turn the bookmark piece right side out through the opening.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Fold press
steps.content:
Use steam for a sharper fold.
Keep the folded edges aligned with the seam line.
Fold the seam allowance at the turning opening inward and press with an iron to hold the edge in place.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Edge stitch
steps.content:
Use the presser foot edge as a guide.
Maintain an even distance from the edge for a neat appearance.
Sew a visible topstitch 0.2 cm around the bookmark edge to secure the opening and finish the shape.

tip.text: Press seams as you go using appropriate heat to set stitches and improve final garment structure.

steps.title: Hand stitch
steps.content:
Use small stitches for clean embroidery details.
Plan the placement before stitching the facial features.
Embroider the eyes and mouth using a hand needle and thread to complete the face details.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:

You may add batting or fusible interfacing inside to create a more structured ear.

steps.title: Repeat sew
steps.content:
Sew each pair evenly for symmetrical ears.

Repeat the same sewing process for the remaining ear pieces to create both ears.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.content:
Use a blunt tool to shape curves.
Shape the curves gently before pressing.
Turn the ears right side out and press them flat to shape.

steps.title: Repeat sew

steps.title: Turn out

Gently push out the curves to form the shape.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Fill stuff
steps.content:
Add stuffing gradually and evenly.
Do not overfill to keep the arms flexible.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling—add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly to prevent seam strain.

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:
Sew carefully along curves.
Keep the opening for turning later.
Place the upper nose pieces right sides facing and sew around the edges, leaving the top open.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Use a blunt stick to shape.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:

steps.title: Fill stuff

tip.text: Avoid overfilling—add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly to prevent seam strain.

steps.title: Align sew
steps.content:
Pin along curves before stitching.
Match the pattern numbers carefully
before sewing.
Align the head front pieces according to the numbered edges and sew them together.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Attach sew
steps.content:
Baste in place if needed before final sewing.
Position pieces carefully before stitching.
Sew the nose and teeth onto the head front piece following the marked placement lines.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Align sew
steps.content:
Check alignment before stitching.
Ensure the seam lines match the front head section.
Align the head back pieces following the numbered edges and sew them together.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Shape curves carefully.

Turn the head back piece right side out after sewing.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:
Secure ears with pins before sewing.
Place the ears between the front and back head layers.
Layer the head front piece, ears, and head back piece in order and sew around the outer edge, leaving the neck opening unsewn.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Use a turning tool gently.
Carefully shape the ears and head curves.
Turn the completed head right side out through the
neck opening.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Attach sew
steps.content:
Mark placement lightly before sewing.
Check position before final stitching.
Sew the decorative heart onto the body following the pattern placement.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Seam sew
steps.content:
Mark the opening before sewing.
Remember to leave the turning gap unsewn.
Place the body front and back pieces right sides facing and sew around the edges, leaving an 8 cm turning opening.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Join sew
steps.content:
Pin around the neck to stabilize.
Ensure the head is centered before sewing.
Insert the head into the body opening and sew around the neck seam to attach the head to the body

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Smooth seams after turning.
Shape the body and head carefully.
Turn the entire doll right side out through the body opening.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Fill stuff
steps.content:
Add stuffing gradually.
Do not overfill to avoid stressing seams.
Insert stuffing evenly into the body until the shape is full and balanced.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling—add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly to prevent seam strain.

steps.title: Hand close
steps.content:
Ladder stitch creates an invisible seam.
Use small stitches for a clean finish.
Close the turning opening with hand stitching.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Attach stitch
steps.content:
Sew through strong fabric layers.
Check placement and symmetry before sewing.
Hand stitch the arms securely onto the sides of the body.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Embroider attach

Use tight small stitches for embroidery.

steps.content:
Use a safety pin to guide the eslastic
Insert elastic throgh the side seam opening

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Knot
steps.content:
Test the tension before fixing
Tie or stitch two ends of the elastic together to secure it

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: hand sew
steps.content:
Stitch invisibly for a neat finish
Hand sew the two remaining openings closed

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

Seam allowance is already included. Cut 2 pieces

Keep stitch spacing even around all sides

steps.title: Prepare the upper panel
steps.content: Cut several 3cm wide strips of fabric, long enough to fit two uppers across.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare the upper panel
steps.content: Sew the strips together to make a panel.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare the upper panel
steps.content: Press and topstitch the panel to coloured felt.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare the upper panel
steps.content: Cut two identical uppers from the panel.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Assemble the sole
steps.content: Snip two soles from patterned fabric, foam wadding, and plain fabric.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the sole
steps.content: Tack together with the foam in the centre and right sides facing out.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach upper to sole and finish
steps.content: Pin the centre of each upper to the centre of the soles before tacking the rest of the edges together.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach upper to sole and finish
steps.content: Cut 3cm strips of fabric and join end to end.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Attach upper to sole and finish
steps.content: Fold under the short end and pin face down around the edge of each slipper.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Prepare the Silk
steps.content: Cover your work surface and protect your clothes.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare the Silk
steps.content: Test the fabric paints on scrap paper.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare the Silk
steps.content: Trim 10cm x 56cm of white silk.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare the Silk
steps.content: Gather a little of the silk into a point and wrap the gathered fabric tightly with an elastic band.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare the Silk
steps.content: Repeat this step six to eight times along the length of the silk.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Apply the Fabric Paint
steps.content: Place a double thickness of kitchen towel or tissues on your covered work surface.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Apply the Fabric Paint
steps.content: Place the silk on top with the elastic bands and gathered fabric facing up.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Apply the Fabric Paint
steps.content: Hold the paint 20cm from the fabric and spray gently until you have the shade and coverage you want.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Apply the Fabric Paint
steps.content: Remove the elastic bands.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Press and Prepare for Sewing
steps.content: When the silk is dry, press flat with an iron at the right setting for the fabric.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Press and Prepare for Sewing
steps.content: Turn one of the short ends over at 1.5cm and press.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Press and Prepare for Sewing
steps.content: Fold the silk in half lengthwise and press again.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Press and Prepare for Sewing
steps.content: Sew along the long raw edge leaving a seam of about 5mm.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Insert the Elastic
steps.content: Turn the fabric inside out so the sewn seam is on the inside

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Insert the Elastic
steps.content: Trim 23cm of 5mm wide elastic.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Insert the Elastic
steps.content: Attach a safety pin to one end.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Insert the Elastic
steps.content: Thread the elastic through the silk, knot the ends very firmly and remove the safety pin.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Insert the Elastic
steps.content: Tuck the raw edge of the silk inside the folded end, then press and sew.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Cut and Prepare Wing Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Cut and Prepare Wing Pieces
steps.content: Cut two large wing shapes from fabric, right sides together, adding a 5mm seam allowance.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Cut and Prepare Wing Pieces
steps.content: Cut two smaller mirrored wings from cotton backed with appliqué adhesive, without seam allowance.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Assemble and Fuse Wings
steps.content: Mount one pair of large wings onto quilt wadding and zig zag stitch together.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble and Fuse Wings
steps.content: Position the smaller wings in the centre of the larger ones and fuse in place with an iron.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble and Fuse Wings
steps.content: Secure by sewing around the edges using a tight zig zag stitch and coordinating thread

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Wing Pieces
steps.content: Layer the remaining large wing pieces over the appliquéd ones, right sides together

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach Wing Pieces
steps.content: Sew around the edges, leaving a small gap in the centre of the straight section on each one.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Edges and Turn
steps.content: Clip the curves and corners.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish Edges and Turn
steps.content: Turn them out with a knitting needle.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finish Edges and Turn
steps.content: Fold the raw edges of the gap and press each wing

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Finish Edges and Turn
steps.content: Oversew the gap with tiny stitches.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add Eyelets
steps.content: Mark three spots down the straight part of each wing, 8mm from the edge and 1.2cm apart, starting from the centre.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Add Eyelets
steps.content: Use a hole punch to make small holes in the wings.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Add Eyelets
steps.content: Fit coloured eyelets into each hole so they can be threaded onto boot laces

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare Pieces
steps.content: Cut two body pieces and two top pieces from patterned fabrics

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare Pieces
steps.content: Cut two fins and one tail piece from fabric folded right sides together.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare Pieces
steps.content: Layer the fins and tail onto wadding and sew around, leaving the straight sections open.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare Pieces
steps.content: Clip the curves, turn each piece out, and press.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Attach Fins and Top Pieces
steps.content: Tack a fin to the top centre of each body piece.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach Fins and Top Pieces
steps.content: Join the top piece to the body piece right sides together along the straight edge.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach Fins and Top Pieces
steps.content: Fold out, press, and topstitch 1mm from the seam

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Fins and Top Pieces
steps.content: Cut two pieces of lining fabric to match the completed body pieces

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Assemble Bag
steps.content: Join the linings to the top of each body piece, stitching across the top and 4cm down each side.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble Bag
steps.content: Clip the corners and turn out the flaps at the top fully.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Assemble Bag
steps.content: Tack the tail piece to the bottom of one body piece, matching up raw edges.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble Bag
steps.content: Pin both body pieces right sides together, matching up the flap seams.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Assemble Bag
steps.content: Sew all around, leaving a 7cm gap in the bottom edge of the lining.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble Bag
steps.content: Turn out through this gap.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Create Drawstring Channels
steps.content: Fold in the raw edges of the gap and slip-stitch closed.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create Drawstring Channels
steps.content: Push the lining into the body.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create Drawstring Channels
steps.content: Fold over half the depth of the top flaps and topstitch to make two channels.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create Drawstring Channels
steps.content: Cut two strips of fabric, 3cm x 30cm each.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Create Drawstring Channels
steps.content: Fold in half lengthways and sew from the centre point down to one end, tapering the stitch line as you reach the end.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create Drawstring Channels
steps.content: Sew the other side in the same way, starting 3cm from the initial centre point.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create Drawstring Channels
steps.content: Turn the tube out through the gap in the middle.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finish Bag
steps.content: Fold in the raw edges of the gap and slip-stitch closed, then press

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Bag
steps.content: Thread a tube through each channel at the top of the bag and knot together on the outside with a reef knot so the top of the bag can be gathered.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish Bag
steps.content: Add buttons to either side of the bag to create eyes.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare fabric and templates
steps.content: Download and print the templates.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare fabric and templates
steps.content: Cut red spot fabric, 52cm × 74cm, and repeat with lining material.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare fabric and templates
steps.content: Iron the red piece in half longways to find the centre.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare fabric and templates
steps.content: Trace the teddy and pyjama templates onto fusible webbing.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare fabric and templates
steps.content: Roughly cut around them and iron one side to the back of grey and blue spot fabric.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare fabric and templates
steps.content: Trim the pieces neatly.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach teddy and pyjamas
steps.content: Iron the teddy in place in the centre of one half of the red spot fabric, moving it 1cm towards the fold

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Attach teddy and pyjamas
steps.content: Machine appliqué in place using grey thread.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach teddy and pyjamas
steps.content: Repeat for the pyjamas.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider details and add nose
steps.content: Attach a quilting foot to your machine and change the thread to black

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider details and add nose
steps.content: Free-hand machine embroider details onto the pyjamas.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider details and add nose
steps.content: Cut out a small black felt nose and sew in place

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble the bag
steps.content: Place the lining along the top of the pyjama bag, right sides together.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the bag
steps.content: Press open the seam.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Assemble the bag
steps.content: Fold the whole bag and lining lengthways in half, right sides together

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Assemble the bag
steps.content: Pin in place

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Assemble the bag
steps.content: Starting from the folded red spot corner, sew along the bottom in white thread, up the side, onto the lining side, and when you join the bottom of the lining, leave a 5cm to 10cm gap

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish the bag
steps.content: Pull the entire bag the right way out through the gap.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish the bag
steps.content: Sew up the gap and press flat

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish the bag
steps.content: Fold the lining inside the main bag and iron neatly in place.

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Finish the bag
steps.content: Stitch a channel, 10cm from the very top edge of the bag.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish the bag
steps.content: Snip a small hole into the seams to gain access.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish the bag
steps.content: Attach a safety pin to the end of cotton tape and feed it through the channel.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Finish the bag
steps.content: Tie a knot to stop the drawstring being pulled back through and press flat to finish.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Cut fabric pieces
steps.content: Allocate a letter to each fabric: A for background, B for center face, and C for ears.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Cut fabric pieces
steps.content: From A, cut one 6.5cm x 16cm rectangle, one 6cm square cut in half diagonally, two 2.5cm x 16cm strips, and two 2.5cm x 19cm strips.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Cut fabric pieces
steps.content: From B, cut one 5.5cm x 16cm rectangle and one 5.5cm x 6.5cm rectangle

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Cut fabric pieces
steps.content: From C, cut two 6.5cm squares and one 7cm square cut in half diagonally.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Assemble the center strip
steps.content: Join the smaller B rectangle to the two 6.5cm squares of C along the longer edge, right sides together, so it sits in the middle.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create the bottom strip
steps.content: Lay the two 6cm triangles of A face down onto the large rectangle of B, aligning one straight side of each with the top edge of the rectangle, leaving a triangle of B in each bottom corner.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Create the bottom strip
steps.content: Stitch along the diagonal and press the A triangles back over the seam.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the top strip
steps.content: Lay the two 7cm triangles of C onto the rectangle of A in the same way as before

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create the top strip
steps.content: Stitch along the diagonal and press the triangles back over the seam.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble the cat face block
steps.content: Stitch the three strips right sides together, matching the bottom of the ears to the center seams of the face.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble the cat face block
steps.content: Press the block well, ensuring the triangles are sitting flat

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Assemble the cat face block
steps.content: Sew a short strip of A to the top and bottom of the block and add the longer strips to the sides to make an overlapped border

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Make seven cat face blocks.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Take eight 20cm squares of coordinating fabric (two of each of four prints) and cut into 10cm squares

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Pair up two squares of two different prints and sew them together in a Battenberg style block. Do the same with the other two prints to create eight blocks in total.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Alternate the cat and Battenberg blocks in a three by five layout and stitch the blocks together in strips of three before sewing the strips together, matching up seams accurately

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Press the quilt top, then layer onto wadding and backing fabric.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Quilt the layers along the main seam lines.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Topstitch around the edge of the quilt, then trim away excess wadding and backing fabric.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Cut several 3.5cm strips of printed fabric and stitch end to end to make a continuous piece long enough to bind the entire quilt.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Sew the binding to the reverse of the quilt with a 5mm seam allowance, mitring the corners.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Quilt
steps.content: Fold the binding over the edges to the front, then under 5mm on the raw edge and topstitch to the front of the quilt, 2mm from the inner edge.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Embroider the motif
steps.content: Download the template, print it, and draw the pattern pieces onto the medium-weight fabric.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider the motif
steps.content: Transfer the motif to the fabric, positioning it centrally on one of the side pieces of the hat to form the main part of the crown

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider the motif
steps.content: Place the fabric in the embroidery hoop, with the design positioned in the centre of the hoop, making sure the fabric is taut but not over-stretched

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider the crab
steps.content: Thread the needle with two strands of coral embroidery thread and fill in the shapes using satin stitch, starting at the centre and working outwards.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Embroider the crab
steps.content: For the top edge of the shell, change to apricot thread, and for the tips of the claws and legs, use black.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider the crab
steps.content: For the starfish, use the same apricot thread, working each section separately, from the centre to the tip of each leg

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider the fish, seaweed and bubbles
steps.content: For the two fish, start with the body, threading the needle with two strands of the blue/green variegated thread and embroidering each scale, starting from the row of scales behind the head and working towards the tail

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider the fish, seaweed and bubbles
steps.content: Using pale blue thread, fill in the head, tail and fins

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider the fish, seaweed and bubbles
steps.content: Complete the seaweed with green and the pebbles with ochre

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Embroider the fish, seaweed and bubbles
steps.content: Using the variegated pastel thread, embroider the bubbles, outlining each one in split stitch.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Embroider the fish, seaweed and bubbles
steps.content: Remove the fabric from the hoop, press lightly on the reverse, then cut out all of the pattern pieces

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Assemble the crown
steps.content: With right sides together, join the side seams on the front and back to form the main part of the crown. Iron the seams open

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the crown
steps.content: Repeat with the two lining pieces.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the crown
steps.content: Pin the top edge of the crown piece to the outer edge of the matching circle, ensuring the wrong sides are facing out. Sew together and clip the curves.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble the crown
steps.content: Repeat this process with the lining.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the brim
steps.content: Place the two brim pieces, right sides together, then join on both short sides and iron the seam allowances open

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the brim
steps.content: Repeat with the lining

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the brim
steps.content: Join the crown to the brim by placing the right sides together with the seams matching, then pin both layers in place and stitch

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble the brim
steps.content: Repeat this step for the lining. Iron the seam allowances to one side, away from the brim

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finalize the hat
steps.content: Turn the main hat right sides out, place the lining into the main hat so that the right sides are pressed together and the wrong sides are facing out. Match the seams.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finalize the hat
steps.content: Pin the two together all round the outer edge of the brim. Stitch, leaving a 6cm gap for turning

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finalize the hat
steps.content: Turn the hat through the opening. Press the brim, then push the seam allowance to the inside on the opening and slip-stitch the folded edges together to close the gap

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finalize the hat
steps.content: Topstitch the edge of the brim close to the edge. Add more lines of topstitching, evenly spaced.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Download, print and cut out the templates.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Cut two 10cm x 15cm pieces each from yellow and cream fabric

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Assemble Baby Blankie
steps.content: Take one yellow and one cream fabric piece, place right sides together, and sew along one of the 15cm edges.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble Baby Blankie
steps.content: Repeat with the remaining fabric pieces

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble Baby Blankie
steps.content: Open both assembled pieces and lay them on top of each other, right sides facing and seams matching.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble Baby Blankie
steps.content: Pin together.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Create Goose Head Outline
steps.content: Mark the goose head template on one side, placing the beak on the yellow side and the head on the cream side.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create Goose Head Outline
steps.content: Align the template so the beak's vertical line meets directly over the seam line

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Create Goose Head Outline
steps.content: Stitch together directly over the marked lines, leaving the bottom straight edge open

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create Goose Head Outline
steps.content: Leave 5cm open at the back of the head for eye stitching.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Goose Head
steps.content: Trim around the head, leaving a 1cm seam allowance

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish Goose Head
steps.content: Turn right side out

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finish Goose Head
steps.content: Embroider eyes on either side of the head, making small, strong, and securing stitches

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Goose Head
steps.content: Turn to the wrong side and finish the remaining 5cm stitching at the back of the head

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finish Goose Head
steps.content: Turn right side out again and fill with toy filling

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Close Neck and Prepare Foot
steps.content: Secure the thread at one side of the open neck and make running stitches around the neck.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Close Neck and Prepare Foot
steps.content: Pull the thread to gather and close the neck gap, tucking in the raw edge

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Close Neck and Prepare Foot
steps.content: Make multiple stitches to secure.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Close Neck and Prepare Foot
steps.content: Lay two pieces of yellow fabric together, right sides facing, pin, and mark a goose foot template.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Close Neck and Prepare Foot
steps.content: Stitch along the marked lines, leaving the straight edge open

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Close Neck and Prepare Foot
steps.content: Trim, snip seam allowance at curves, and turn right side out.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.
Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble Goose Body
steps.content: Cut two body pieces from the template.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Assemble Goose Body
steps.content: Lay one body piece flat, right side up

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble Goose Body
steps.content: Position a foot along each straight edge at the bottom of the body, with the foot facing inwards.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble Goose Body
steps.content: Tack or pin in place.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Assemble Goose Body
steps.content: Lay the second body piece on top, right side down

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble Goose Body
steps.content: Sew all around, leaving the top neck edge open.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble Goose Body
steps.content: Turn right side out.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Attach Head to Body
steps.content: Close the neck gap with a long running stitch

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Head to Body
steps.content: Ladder stitch the head to the neck securely.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Cut circles
steps.content: Cut two 30cm diameter circles from printed cotton, wadding, and lining.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Layer and stitch circles
steps.content: Layer both sets with right sides facing out and the wadding in the middle.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Layer and stitch circles
steps.content: Zig zag stitch around the edge.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Cut and prepare opening
steps.content: Cut across one of the circles approximately a third of the way down

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Cut and prepare opening
steps.content: Zig zag stitch along the straight edges

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach zip
steps.content: Sew the straight edges, right sides together, to each edge of a centrally positioned 30cm zip.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach zip
steps.content: Trim the seam allowance on the fabric layers to 2mm.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach zip
steps.content: Fold the zip tape over the raw edges and topstitch in place.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Secure zip ends
steps.content: Zig zag stitch over each end of the zip to shorten to the circle width.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Secure zip ends
steps.content: Cut away the excess.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Assemble case
steps.content: Close the zip halfway, then sew the two circles together.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add binding
steps.content: Add binding to the case back, flip it over to the front, and slip-stitch in place

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare pattern pieces and fabric
steps.content: Download the templates and cut out according to the cutting guide.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare pattern pieces and fabric
steps.content: Pin and stitch the pin tucks on the front, as marked on the pattern pieces

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare pattern pieces and fabric
steps.content: Staystitch the neckline and armholes on each front and back piece, 0.5cm from the edge

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Join back pieces
steps.content: With right sides together, pin the two back vest pieces together along the centre back seam.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Join back pieces
steps.content: Using a 1.5cm seam allowance, stitch from the bottom and stop 10cm from the top.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Join back pieces
steps.content: Back-stitch to secure and press the seam open

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach front and back pieces
steps.content: Pin the front and back vest pieces together, right sides facing, and align the shoulder and side seams.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach front and back pieces
steps.content: Stitch together with a 1.5cm seam allowance

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare bias binding
steps.content: Using a bias binding maker, press your strips of fabric into 2.4cm lengths and roll in half to create a double-fold tape

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Bind the neckline
steps.content: Starting from one of the centre back seams, fold the short end of the bias tape over by 0.9cm to ensure the raw edges are hidden

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Bind the neckline
steps.content: Slip the neckline between the folds of the bias binding, placing the raw edge against the centre crease

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Bind the neckline
steps.content: Stitch the bias tape in place from the outside, making sure to catch in the folded edge on the underside, then fold the ends of the binding under to complete.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Bind the neckline
steps.content: Press in place.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Bind the armholes
steps.content: Starting at one of the side seams, bind the armhole by folding the short end of the bias tape over by 0.9cm to hide the raw edges

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Bind the armholes
steps.content: Slip the armhole between the folds of the bias binding, placing the raw edge against the centre crease.

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Bind the armholes
steps.content: Sew the bias tape from the outside, catching in the folded edge on the underside and overlap the ends of the binding to complete, then press

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add button and buttonhole
steps.content: Stitch a horizontal buttonhole in the bias binding to the left of the centre back.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add button and buttonhole
steps.content: Sew a button on the bias binding to the right of the centre back

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish
steps.content: Press the garment

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Prepare main panel
steps.content: Cut rectangles from printed fabric, plain cotton and wadding, 36cm × 48cm

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare main panel
steps.content: Sandwich the wadding in between the two pieces of fabric, wrong sides together, and pin.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Prepare main panel
steps.content: Stitch a line across one short end, 7.5cm from the top edge

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare pocket panel
steps.content: Trim rectangles from plain cotton, printed fabric and wadding, 29cm × 36cm, and layer them together.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare pocket panel
steps.content: Snip a 3cm wide strip of plain cotton and bind the top long edge of the layers.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare pocket panel
steps.content: Stitch to the reverse first, fold over, then fold under 5mm of the raw edge, and topstitch to the front.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach pocket panel to main panel
steps.content: Pin the layers to the background panel, lining up the bottom edges.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Prepare small pockets
steps.content: Cut rectangles from plain cotton, printed fabric and wadding, 14cm × 36cm, then layer and bind the top edge.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare small pockets
steps.content: Cut two 12cm squares from printed and plain fabric, then pin right sides together

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare small pockets
steps.content: Sew down both sides of each set and turn out

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Prepare small pockets
steps.content: Bind the top edges of the squares.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach small pockets to pocket panel
steps.content: Pin and topstitch the pockets to the smallest layered rectangle, lining up the bottom edges.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach small pockets to pocket panel
steps.content: Pin the finished rectangle to the bottom edge of the main panel.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Secure pocket panels
steps.content: Secure all of the pocket panels in place by stitching around the sides, 5mm in from the edges, leaving a 4cm gap in the top edge, 4cm from a corner.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Bind edges
steps.content: Trim the seam allowance to 3mm before binding the edges using 3cm strips of fabric.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Bind edges
steps.content: Allow the binding to run over the gap, but do not stitch it in place.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add weight
steps.content: Make a funnel from a sheet of paper and slide the end into the gap so that it sits in between the backing fabric and wadding

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Add weight
steps.content: Fill the stitched channel three quarters full with dry rice or weighted pellets

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add weight
steps.content: Fold in the edges of the unsewn binding and stitch the gap closed.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish
steps.content: Use the weighted channel to hang the tidy over the back or arm of a chair

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare umbrella shapes
steps.content: Download and print the templates.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare umbrella shapes
steps.content: Trace and cut out four three-part umbrella shapes from interfacing

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare umbrella shapes
steps.content: Trim four handles from coloured felt

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare umbrella shapes
steps.content: Choose three similar shades of cotton print fabric and iron an umbrella section to the reverse of each.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create umbrella blocks
steps.content: Cut around the interfacing to leave a 7mm border.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Create umbrella blocks
steps.content: Fold the excess fabric over the edges of the interfacing and tack down, clipping curves and mitring corners where necessary.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Create umbrella blocks
steps.content: Place the tacked pieces right sides together and oversew to join them.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create umbrella blocks
steps.content: Press the shape flat but do not remove the tacking.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Add handle
steps.content: Cut a 16cm square of a contrasting colour print fabric and fold in half diagonally one way and then the other, finger-pressing the folds.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Add handle
steps.content: Lay a felt umbrella handle along one line, starting from the middle of the block.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Add handle
steps.content: Oversew in place with two strands of matching embroidery thread

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete umbrella blocks
steps.content: Pin and oversew the top of the umbrella to cover the top end of the handle, using the pressed lines to position it centrally.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete umbrella blocks
steps.content: Make three more blocks in the same way, creating two of each colourway

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create bordered block
steps.content: Create a block using two different colours as described above.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create bordered block
steps.content: Cut 3cm wide strips of a darker fabric and use these to make an overlapped border all around.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Add fabric squares
steps.content: Cut 28, 6.5cm squares of coloured fabric.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Add fabric squares
steps.content: Sew two sets of six squares together in a line and stitch these to the top and bottom edges of the block.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add fabric squares
steps.content: Sew the remaining squares into two sets of eight and stitch these down either side, carefully matching the seams at the corners.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add fabric squares
steps.content: Press the panel.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Create bag lining
steps.content: Pin the panel, face up, onto a 46cm square of heavyweight cotton or blackout material and topstitch along the seam lines.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create bag lining
steps.content: Cut a second square of heavyweight fabric and stitch to the panel, right sides together, along three sides.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create drawstring channel
steps.content: Cut 4.5cm wide strips of fabric and join end to end to make a piece 97cm long.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Create drawstring channel
steps.content: Fold in the short ends by 5mm and hem.

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Create drawstring channel
steps.content: Place the strip along the top edge, right side against the inside of the bag and stitch in place, making sure the hemmed ends meet at one side

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add drawstring
steps.content: Flip the strip out of the bag, turn under 5mm of the raw edge, fold down and topstitch over the previous seam to make a channel.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Add drawstring
steps.content: Thread a safety pin through a length of cord and use as a needle to pass the cord through the channel to make a drawstring.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Prepare Half Square Triangles (HSTs)
steps.content: Download and print templates.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare Half Square Triangles (HSTs)
steps.content: Take three yellow 7.5cm squares and draw a diagonal line on the back of each

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare Half Square Triangles (HSTs)
steps.content: Pair each yellow square with a green 7.5cm square, right sides together.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare Half Square Triangles (HSTs)
steps.content: Stitch 4mm either side of the drawn line.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare Half Square Triangles (HSTs)
steps.content: Cut along the line, open out and press the resulting half square triangles (HSTs)

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare Half Square Triangles (HSTs)
steps.content: Trim to 7cm square if necessary.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the Trunk
steps.content: Stitch the two background 7cm x 10cm rectangles to either side of the red square.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Lower Tree Section
steps.content: Sew two 7cm green squares together.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Lower Tree Section
steps.content: Add a HST on either side to make the lower portion of the tree.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create the Lower Tree Section
steps.content: Sew the bottom of this strip to the trunk strip, lining up the central seam with the centre of the trunk and press.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Make the Middle Tree Section
steps.content: Sew a HST to either side of the remaining 7cm green square.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Make the Middle Tree Section
steps.content: Stitch the smaller yellow 7cm x 4cm rectangles to each end to make the middle section of the tree

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Form the Top of the Tree
steps.content: Stitch the last two HSTs together to make a point.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Form the Top of the Tree
steps.content: Add the 7cm yellow squares to each end to make the top of the tree.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Form the Top of the Tree
steps.content: Sew this strip to the middle section, lining up the centre seam with the middle of the square and press.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete the Tree Block
steps.content: Sew the two pieces together to complete the tree and press well.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete the Tree Block
steps.content: Use a quilter’s rule to square up the block to 26cm square.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Complete the Tree Block
steps.content: Make five more blocks in the same way using a variety of greens and blues

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the Quilt Top (Optional)
steps.content: If you wish to make a quilt with just the tree design, continue until you’ve made 12 blocks

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the Quilt Top (Optional)
steps.content: Cut several 3cm wide strips of printed fabric to use as sashing.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Assemble the Quilt Top (Optional)
steps.content: Lay out the blocks and add sashing between each one to make rows.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Assemble the Quilt Top (Optional)
steps.content: Join the rows with longer pieces of sashing and press well.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Layer and Topstitch
steps.content: Pin the quilt top onto wadding and background fabric, leaving an excess of at least 2cm all round.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Layer and Topstitch
steps.content: Topstitch the quilt top to the backing layers along one side of the sashing, stitching in the ditch.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Layer and Topstitch
steps.content: Smooth out and pin the layers together along the edges of the quilt, before topstitching 2mm from the edge and trimming away the excess backing.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Bind the Edges
steps.content: Cut more 3cm wide strips of cotton print fabric and join end to end to make a continuous piece long enough to bind the edges of the quilt.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Bind the Edges
steps.content: Sew the binding to the reverse of the quilt, matching up the raw edges and mitring the corner.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Bind the Edges
steps.content: Join the ends of the binding neatly on one long side and trim away the excess length.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Bind the Edges
steps.content: Flip the binding over to the front of the quilt, fold under 5mm on the raw edge.

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Bind the Edges
steps.content: Pin and topstitch to the top of the quilt, covering any previous seams and stitching 2mm from the inner folded edge

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare the body pieces
steps.content: Download and print the pattern.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare the body pieces
steps.content: Cut two body pieces from fabric.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare the body pieces
steps.content: Place right sides together and pin the two side edges.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Prepare the body pieces
steps.content: Sew a 1cm seam on each side, and press out.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare the body pieces
steps.content: Zig zag or overlock the edges if required.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create the leg holes
steps.content: With right sides still facing in, fold the edge of the leg section over by 1cm, then 1cm again

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Create the leg holes
steps.content: Press, pin and topstitch the hem close to the edge.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create the leg holes
steps.content: Cut 30cm of 4mm wide elastic, attach a small safety pin to one end and thread it through the channel.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Create the leg holes

steps.title: Attach Front Body

steps.title: Box Corners
steps.content: Box both corners by 6cm: pull opposite sides of the fabric apart at a corner and align the seams to form a triangular shape

steps.title: Box Corners
steps.content: Measure and mark 6cm down from the corner point on one side along one seam.

steps.title: Box Corners
steps.content: Draw a horizontal line to the left and right of this mark, forming a triangle shape.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Box Corners
steps.content: Stitch along this line and trim the excess. Repeat for the other corner and turn right side out.

steps.title: Assemble Lining
steps.content: With right sides facing, pin and sew the front piece to the bottom half of the main body piece, stitching around the three sides and leaving the hemmed edge open.

steps.title: Assemble Lining
