items.name: Sweetheart Dino Plush

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 2 pieces for the dinosaur body following the paper pattern. These pieces form the main front and back of the project.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 1 piece for the dinosaur top section following the paper pattern. This piece forms the upper decorative layer.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 1 piece for the dinosaur bottom section following the paper pattern. This piece completes the body structure.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 6 small heart pieces for the dinosaur decoration using the paper pattern. These will be attached as decorative details.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance is already included.
Cut 5 decorative heart pieces following the paper pattern. These pieces will be used as embellishments.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:
Sew slowly around curves to keep the heart shape neat.
Do not sew the bottom opening so the piece can be turned later.
Place two heart pieces right sides facing and sew around the edges, leaving the bottom edge open. Repeat for all heart units.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Use a blunt tool to shape curves without damaging fabric.
Gently push out the curves for a clean heart shape.
Turn each heart piece right side out through the bottom opening and shape the edges

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Join sew
steps.content:
Pin along the seam to prevent shifting.
Match the center points before sewing to keep the shape balanced.
Align the dinosaur upper piece and lower piece, then sew along the connecting seam to join them together.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Seam sew
steps.content:
Sew slowly along curves for smoother results.
Keep edges aligned while sewing curved sections.
Sew the dinosaur body side pieces to the joined upper and lower body section to form the full body shape

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Insert sew
steps.content:
Clip curves before turning to reduce bulk.
Remember to leave a 5 cm opening for turning and stuffing.
Place the finished heart pieces inside the dinosaur body following the marked positions. Sew around the dinosaur body edge and leave a 5 cm turning opening on the back.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Use a blunt stick to shape edges.
Push out corners and curves gently for a smooth shape.
Turn the dinosaur body right side out through the turning opening

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Fill stuff
steps.content:
Add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly.
Do not overfill, or the seams may become strained.
Insert stuffing through the opening until the dinosaur body is evenly filled.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling—add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly to prevent seam strain.

steps.title: Hand close
steps.content:
Use ladder stitch for an invisible seam.
Keep stitches small and tight for a neat finish.
Close the turning opening with hand stitching to secure the stuffing inside.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Attach stitch
steps.content:
Use small stitches to keep the decoration secure.
Check placement before sewing permanently.
Hand stitch the decorative heart onto the dinosaur body at the desired position.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut

Seam allowance of 0.5 cm is already included
Cut 2 pieces for the bookmark piece following the paper pattern. These will form the hanging bookmark section.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Align sew
steps.content:
Use pins to keep small pieces aligned.
Match the pattern numbers carefully before sewing to ensure correct assembly.
Align the upper head, lower head, and bookmark pieces according to the numbered pattern edges, then sew them together to form the front side.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Seam sew
steps.content:
Check alignment before stitching.
Make sure the seam lines match the front side for a balanced shape.
Repeat the same sewing process for the back side pieces following the same numbered edges.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:
Mark the turning opening before sewing.
Do not sew the 4 cm turning gap so the piece can be turned later.
Place the two assembled bookmark pieces right sides facing and sew around the edges, leaving a 4 cm turning opening.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.content:
Use a blunt tool to shape corners.
Gently push out edges and curves to shape the piece neatly.
Turn the bookmark piece right side out through the opening.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Fold press
steps.content:
Use steam for a sharper fold.
Keep the folded edges aligned with the seam line.
Fold the seam allowance at the turning opening inward and press with an iron to hold the edge in place.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Edge stitch
steps.content:
Use the presser foot edge as a guide.
Maintain an even distance from the edge for a neat appearance.
Sew a visible topstitch 0.2 cm around the bookmark edge to secure the opening and finish the shape.

tip.text: Press seams as you go using appropriate heat to set stitches and improve final garment structure.

steps.title: Hand stitch
steps.content:
Use small stitches for clean embroidery details.
Plan the placement before stitching the facial features.
Embroider the eyes and mouth using a hand needle and thread to complete the face details.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

Seam allowance of 1 cm is already included
Cut 2 tail pieces using pattern piece 7. These pieces
will be sewn together to create the tail.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:
Sew carefully along curves.
Keep the top opening for turning later.
Place the ear pieces right sides facing and sew around
the edges leaving the top opening unsewn.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Turn press
steps.content:
Use steam for a clean finish.
Smooth the seams before pressing.
Turn the ears right side out and press them flat to shape.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Seam sew
steps.content:
Pin edges before sewing.
Keep the bottom opening for turning and stuffing.
Align the tail pieces right sides facing and sew around
the edges leaving the bottom opening unsewn.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Use a blunt tool to push out corners.
Shape the curves gently after turning.
Turn the tail right side out through the
bottom opening.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Fill stuff
steps.content:
Distribute stuffing evenly.
Avoid overfilling to keep the shape soft.
Insert stuffing through the bottom opening until the tail is evenly filled.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling—add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly to prevent seam strain.

steps.title: Hand attach
steps.content:
Mark the eye positions lightly before stitching.
Check placement before sewing to keep the face balanced.
Hand sew the decorative eyes onto the head and embroider the eyebrow details with thread.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Align sew
steps.content:
Pin the seam to keep curves aligned.
Align the pattern numbers carefully
before sewing.
Sew the lower jaw to the head following the matching
numbered pattern edges.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Align sew
steps.content:
Sew slowly along curved seams.
Ensure seams meet neatly at connection points.
Sew the forehead piece to the head following the matching numbered edges.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Use a turning tool carefully.
Gently shape the curves of the head.
Turn the assembled head right side out through the neck opening.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Seam sew
steps.content:
Pin the seam before sewing.
Match the center points before sewing.
Align the body and belly pieces right sides facing and sew the upper seam to join them.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Repeat sew
steps.content:
Sew slowly along curves.
Keep edges aligned during sewing.
Sew the body side seams following the same method to form the body shape.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Seam sew
steps.content:
Check seam alignment carefully.
Ensure seams meet evenly.
Sew the connection seam between the belly sections to complete the body structure.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Insert sew
steps.content:
Secure the ears with pins before sewing.
Remember to leave the 8 cm opening for turning later.
Place the ears inside the body layers and sew along the back seam, leaving an 8 cm turning opening.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Join sew
steps.content:
Pin around the neck seam to stabilize layers.
Ensure the head is centered before stitching.
Place the head inside the body opening and sew around the neck seam to attach the head to the body.

tip.text: Misalignment is common—pin key points and edges first to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Smooth seams by hand.
Carefully shape the body and head after turning.
Turn the entire piece right side out through the turning opening.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Fill stuff
steps.content:
Distribute stuffing evenly throughout the body.
Fill gradually to keep the shape balanced.
Insert stuffing through the opening until the body is evenly filled.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling—add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly to prevent seam strain.

steps.title: Hand close
steps.content:
Ladder stitch works well for invisible closing.
Use small stitches for a neat finish.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Attach stitch
steps.content:
Secure with small strong stitches.
Check placement for symmetry before sewing.
Hand sew the ears onto the head at the desired position to complete the character.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

items.name: Heart & Star Sachet

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance of 1 cm is already included
Cut 2 star pieces using pattern piece 1. These pieces will form the main star shape when sewn together.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Cut - pattern cut
steps.content:
Seam allowance of 1 cm is already included
Cut 1 heart piece using pattern piece 2. This piece will be used as a decorative element.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and cut in smooth, continuous strokes for clean and accurate edges.

steps.title: Edge sew
steps.content:
Mark the turning gap before sewing.
Remember to keep the 4 cm gap unsewn for turning later.
Place the heart pieces right sides facing and sew around the edge, leaving a 4 cm turning opening.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Turn out
steps.content:
Use a blunt tool to shape edges.
Gently push out the curves to shape the heart neatly.
Turn the heart piece right side out through the opening.

tip.text: Sew slowly along curves and reduce stitch length slightly to prevent puckering and maintain a smooth seam.

steps.title: Fill stuff
steps.content:
Distribute stuffing evenly.
Do not overfill to keep the shape soft and smooth.
Insert stuffing through the opening until the heart is evenly filled.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling—add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly to prevent seam strain.

steps.title: Hand close
steps.content:
Ladder stitch helps hide the seam.
Use small stitches to keep the seam neat and secure.
Close the turning opening with hand stitching.

tip.text: Turn the piece gently using a blunt tool to avoid putting stress on the seams.

steps.title: Tie bow
steps.content:
Adjust the loops evenly for a balanced shape.
A pre-made decorative bow can also be used if preferred.
Tie the ribbon into a decorative bow.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Attach stitch
steps.content:
Use strong stitches to secure the bow.
Check placement before sewing permanently.
Hand sew the bow onto the heart decoration.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Ring attach
steps.content:
Use pliers if the ring is tight.
Ensure the loop is secure so the ring does not slip out.
Pass the metal key ring through the bow loop to attach the keychain hardware.

tip.text: Control fabric movement carefully and sew steadily to maintain accuracy and a professional finish.

steps.title: Repeat sew
steps.content:
Repeat the same method for consistent results.
Keep the sewing opening and stuffing process the same as the heart.
Follow the same construction steps to make the star decoration.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling—add stuffing gradually and distribute evenly to prevent seam strain.

steps.title: Prepare Front Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the pattern and trace off the pieces and cut out from fabric, transferring any pattern markings

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare Front Pieces
steps.content: Along the front edges of the front pieces, fold under and press 2 cm to the wrong side

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Prepare Front Pieces
steps.content: Hand tack across the top and bottom

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Create Button Stand
steps.content: Fold under and press another 2 cm to the wrong side, so that all the raw edges are encased. This forms the button stand

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Create Button Stand
steps.content: Turn the second fold back to the right side

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Create Button Stand
steps.content: Machine stitch across the top of the fold from the centre front to the outside edge

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Create Button Stand
steps.content: Make a small snip from the neck edge to the stitches

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Button Stand
steps.content: Turn the button stand through to the wrong side and re-press the front edge. You should have a finished “step” at the top of the button stand and the raw edge should be completely encased

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finish Button Stand
steps.content: Edge stitch 3 mm from the inner folded edge, securing the button stand in place

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Button Stand
steps.content: Repeat on the opposite front piece

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Gather Front Shoulders
steps.content: Set your sewing machine to a long stitch length

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Gather Front Shoulders
steps.content: Working on the right side between the circles, sew a row of stitches inside the seam allowance at the front shoulders, a fraction away from the seam line, about 1.2cm from the edge

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Gather Front Shoulders
steps.content: Start with a back stitch, but don’t finish the row of stitches with one

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Gather Front Shoulders
steps.content: Sew a second parallel row of stitches still inside the seam allowance, a little way from the first row

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Yoke to Back
steps.content: Working on the wrong side, pull on the bobbin threads, sliding the fabric along until it is the same length as the front edge of the yoke

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach Yoke to Back
steps.content: Once the correct length, draw the threads through to the wrong side and knot them or wrap them around a pin in a figure-of-eight

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach Yoke to Back
steps.content: With right sides together, pin the longer edges of two outer yokes to the back of the blouse, matching up the notches and circles.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Sandwich and Stitch Yokes.
steps.content: Lay the second yoke pieces, which act as facings, over the pinned seams, with the right side of these yoke pieces against the wrong side of the back shoulder seam, effectively sandwiching the back between two yoke layers

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Sandwich and Stitch Yokes
steps.content: Pin in place

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Sandwich and Stitch Yokes
steps.content: Stitch the back shoulder seams, sewing through all three layers

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Sandwich and Stitch Yokes
steps.content: Press the yoke pieces away from the back, so that the seam allowances are sandwiched in between the two

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Sandwich and Stitch Yokes
steps.content: Press under a 1.5cm fold on the front edge of the yoke facing piece

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Attach Gathered Front to Yoke
steps.content: With right sides together and matching up the circles, pin the gathered front seam to the front edge of the yoke piece

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach Gathered Front to Yoke
steps.content: Stitch the front yoke seam; your stitches should be just to the left of the rows of gathering. Repeat on the opposite side of the front

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Gathered Front to Yoke
steps.content: Pin the previously pressed edge of the yoke facing over the gathered front shoulder, lining up the folded edge with the seam line.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach Gathered Front to Yoke
steps.content: Slip stitch it in place

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Gathered Front to Yoke
steps.content: Hand tack around the armhole edge of the yokes. These two layers will now be treated as a single piece

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare Front Neckline and Shoulder Seams
steps.content: Download and print the template, following the cutting guide

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare Front Neckline and Shoulder Seams
steps.content: Set your machine to the largest stitch and sew two parallel rows within the seam allowance between the two notches marked on the front neckline

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare Front Neckline and Shoulder Seams
steps.content: Gather to measure 8cm

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare Front Neckline and Shoulder Seams
steps.content: Neaten the shoulder seam edges on the back and front

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare Front Neckline and Shoulder Seams
steps.content: Match both across the shoulder edges with right sides together, pin, then stitch one of the shoulder seams and press open

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Apply Bias Binding to Neckline
steps.content: Press 1cm on either side of the neck band strip or use a bias binding folder.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Apply Bias Binding to Neckline
steps.content: Open one side and position it around the neckline from each end of the open shoulder

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Apply Bias Binding to Neckline
steps.content: Pin, then sew along the line pressed into the strip

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Apply Bias Binding to Neckline
steps.content: Fold onto the wrong side of the neckline and position the remaining folded edge over the stitching line

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Apply Bias Binding to Neckline
steps.content: Pin and edgestitch in place, sandwiching the raw edge inside the bias binding

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Apply Bias Binding to Neckline
steps.content: Trim away any excess

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Complete Shoulder and Side Seams
steps.content: Sew the remaining shoulder edges, then press the seam open

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete Shoulder and Side Seams
steps.content: Fold the two ends of the binding under into an arrow head shape and stitch in place

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete Shoulder and Side Seams
steps.content: Match the side seams, right sides together. Pin and stitch, then neaten the seam allowances and press towards the back of the top

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare Sleeves for Gathers
steps.content: Adjust the stitch length to number 5, then sew a line around the head of the upper sleeves, starting from the front notch and finishing at the back notches indicated.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare Sleeves for Gathers
steps.content: Make two parallel rows of stitching within the seam allowance between the two notches marked on the lower sleeves, at both the top and base.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Lower Sleeves and Cuffs
steps.content: Pull up the threads on the top edge of the lower sleeve to match the base of the upper one

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach Lower Sleeves and Cuffs
steps.content: Distribute the gathers evenly, then set the machine back to its normal stitch length.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Lower Sleeves and Cuffs
steps.content: Sew the lower sleeve to the upper, then neaten and press the seam towards the top

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Lower Sleeves and Cuffs
steps.content: Pull the threads at the base of the sleeves to fit the 28cm cuff bias strips.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish Sleeves and Attach to Armholes
steps.content: Open one side of the cuff strips and position along the gathered base edge. Pin in place, then stitch along the fold line pressed into the bias strip

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Sleeves and Attach to Armholes
steps.content: Turn the strip onto the wrong side of the sleeves and position the remaining folded edge over the stitching line, then edgestitch in place.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finish Sleeves and Attach to Armholes
steps.content: Neaten the edges, then pin and sew the two edges on both sleeves.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Sleeves and Attach to Armholes
steps.content: Fold the ends of the cuff bindings under into an arrow head shape and stitch in place.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Sleeves and Attach to Armholes
steps.content: Pull the threads up around the head of the sleeve and place into the armholes, matching the notches.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish Sleeves and Attach to Armholes
steps.content: Position the side and underarm seams of the sleeves, then sew in place.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish Sleeves and Attach to Armholes
steps.content: Neaten around the seam allowances of the armholes

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Hem the Blouse
steps.content: Fold and press 1cm around the base, then another 1cm, before edgestitching the hem in place.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare and stitch shoulder seams
steps.content: Download the pattern, print it, and follow the cutting guide

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare and stitch shoulder seams
steps.content: Neaten the side edges of the front and back blouse and across the shoulders

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare and stitch shoulder seams
steps.content: Position the front and back blouse together, right sides facing

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare and stitch shoulder seams
steps.content: Match, pin and stitch just one of the shoulder seams together

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare and stitch shoulder seams
steps.content: Press the seam allowance open

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Bind the neckline
steps.content: Fold and press in 1cm on either side of the bias neck binding or use a bias binding folder

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Bind the neckline
steps.content: Open one side of the neck binding strip and position it around the neckline on the wrong side, starting and finishing on the open shoulder edges

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Bind the neckline
steps.content: Pin in place, then sew along the fold line pressed into the bias strip.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Bind the neckline
steps.content: Fold the bias strip over onto the right side of the neckline and position the remaining folded edge over the stitching line, pin and edgestitch in place, sandwiching the raw edge of the neckline inside the bias binding.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete shoulder seams and stitch side seams
steps.content: Stitch the remaining shoulder seam together and press the seam open

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete shoulder seams and stitch side seams
steps.content: Fold the bound end of the seam under into an arrow shape and sew in place.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete shoulder seams and stitch side seams
steps.content: Match, pin and stitch the back and front blouse side edges together.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Complete shoulder seams and stitch side seams
steps.content: Press the seam allowance open.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Prepare and stitch sleeves
steps.content: Neaten the side edges of both sleeves.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Prepare and stitch sleeves
steps.content: Adjust the stitch length to number 5 and sew two rows of gathering stitches, within the seam allowance, around the head of the sleeves starting and finishing as indicated on the pattern piece

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare and stitch sleeves
steps.content: Match, pin and stitch the underarm sleeve seams together, right sides facing

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare and stitch sleeves
steps.content: Press the seams open and turn the sleeves right side out.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Attach sleeves
steps.content: Pull the gathering threads up in the head of the sleeve then place, matching the notches, into the armholes of the blouse.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach sleeves
steps.content: Match the side seams and the underarm seam of the sleeves together.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach sleeves
steps.content: Distribute the gathers evenly, pin and sew the sleeves in place

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach sleeves
steps.content: Neaten around the seam allowances of the armholes.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Add elastic cuffs
steps.content: Fold and press up 1cm around the base of the sleeves and another 1cm.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Add elastic cuffs
steps.content: Pin and edgestitch in place, leaving a gap in the end of the stitching line

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add elastic cuffs
steps.content: Cut two lengths of elastic that fit comfortably around your wrists

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Add elastic cuffs
steps.content: Use a small safety pin to thread the elastic through the channel formed at the base of the sleeves.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Add elastic cuffs
steps.content: Cross the ends of the elastic over one another and sew to secure

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add elastic cuffs
steps.content: Pull the elastic inside the channel and stitch to seal the gap.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Finish the hem
steps.content: Neaten the base of the blouse, press up the 1cm hem and edgestitch in place.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare the Cuffs and Neckband
steps.content: Download and print the pattern, following the cutting guide.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Prepare the Cuffs and Neckband
steps.content: Apply fusible interfacing to the fold line on each cuff and the neckband

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Assemble the Back Blouse
steps.content: Pin the center-back seam with right sides together, matching notches, and stitch up to the marker for the back opening

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble the Back Blouse
steps.content: Press the seam open and press the back opening along the seam line

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Assemble the Back Blouse
steps.content: Press the seam allowance towards the seam along the length of the center-back, including the opening, and sew in place within the seam allowance, not through to the back blouse.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble the Back Blouse
steps.content: Topstitch around the back opening.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Front and Back at Shoulders
steps.content: Stitch two rows of gathers between the notches on the front blouse neckline

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Front and Back at Shoulders
steps.content: Pin the front and back blouse at the shoulder with right sides together, and sew the seam.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Front and Back at Shoulders
steps.content: Neaten the seam allowance using an overlocker or zig zag stitch

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach Front and Back at Shoulders
steps.content: Press the seam towards the back.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Attach the Neckband
steps.content: Pin the neckband to the neckline of the blouse with right sides together.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach the Neckband
steps.content: Pin the center front in place, then distribute the gathers evenly between the notches.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach the Neckband
steps.content: Pin the center-back notch, level with the edges of the back opening.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach the Neckband
steps.content: The neckband should extend beyond the opening by 4cm.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Attach the Neckband
steps.content: Stitch the neckband in place, remove the gathering threads, and trim the seam, snipping the curved sections where necessary.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach the Neckband
steps.content: Press the seam towards the neckband. Press the neckband along the fold line

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Finish the Neckband and Add Buttonhole
steps.content: Turn the neckband back on itself, right sides together at the center back, and stitch across the ends.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finish the Neckband and Add Buttonhole
steps.content: Trim the seam and turn out the ends of the band to the right side

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finish the Neckband and Add Buttonhole
steps.content: Finish the inside of the neckband by either turning under the seam allowance and slip-stitching in place, or overlocking the edge of the neckband and topstitching in place along the edge.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Finish the Neckband and Add Buttonhole
steps.content: Make a buttonhole on the left side of the back opening and sew a corresponding button on the other side.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach the Sleeves
steps.content: Pin the sleeves in place around the armhole with right sides together, matching all notches and easing where

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach the Sleeves
steps.content: Stitch, then neaten using an overlocker or zig zag stitch.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach the Sleeves
steps.content: Press the seam towards the sleeve.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Assemble Side and Sleeve Seams
steps.content: Pin the blouse side seams and sleeve seams together with right sides together and matching notches, then stitch in one seam from the edge of the sleeve to the hem of the blouse.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble Side and Sleeve Seams
steps.content: Neaten the seam and press towards the back.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Assemble Side and Sleeve Seams
steps.content: Sew two rows of gathering threads around the bottom edge of the sleeve

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach the Cuffs
steps.content: Pin and stitch the ends of the cuff with right sides together.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach the Cuffs
steps.content: Trim the seam and press open.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Attach the Cuffs
steps.content: Fold the cuffs along the fold line, right side out, and press

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Attach the Cuffs
steps.content: Pin the folded cuffs to the bottom of the sleeve with right sides together, matching notches and having the sleeve seam and the cuff seam level. Distribute the gathers evenly

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Attach the Cuffs
steps.content: Stitch the cuff in place and remove the gathering threads.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Attach the Cuffs
steps.content: Neaten the seam using an overlocker or zig zag stitch. Press the seam away from the cuff towards the sleeve.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Hem the Blouse
steps.content: Turn up the hem by 2.5cm and press, then stitch in place.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block
steps.content: Copy your bodice block.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block
steps.content: To lower your neck, lower point J, or make it wider by moving K towards B.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block
steps.content: Add 10cm to the length of line JC, creating point L.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block
steps.content: Square across from L and extend line BH to meet this, creating point M.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block
steps.content: Check that line LM is at least 1cm longer than the top hip measurement divided by 4. If it isn’t, extend line LM from point M until it is.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block
steps.content: Eliminate the underarm curve, creating a slightly looser fit, and mark H with a notch.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block (Alternate)
steps.content: If E on your block is wider than B, follow Step 1.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block (Alternate)
steps.content: Square across from L in a parallel line

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block (Alternate)
steps.content: Create point M so that CE is the same distance plus 2.5cm.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block (Alternate)
steps.content: Join M to B in a diagonal line.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Adjust Bodice Block (Alternate)
steps.content: Transfer point G onto the new line MB and mark it with a notch.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish Bodice
steps.content: Add a 1.5cm seam allowance to all edges except the neckline and foldline, where you add 1cm.

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Finish Bodice
steps.content: Label the bodice ‘FRONT AND BACK BODICE, CUT ONE PAIR’.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Finish Bodice
steps.content: Copy the foldline and the grainline position from the master block

tip.text: Ensure folds are even and aligned to avoid distortion in the final shape.

steps.title: Make the Band
steps.content: Divide your top hip measurement by 2 and add 1cm. Draw a horizontal line that is this measurement (line AB). Mark the center of this line C.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Make the Band
steps.content: Square down 11cm from C, and label point F.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Make the Band
steps.content: Take your lower hip measurement, divide by 4 and add 5mm. Starting from F, measure out this distance to the left and then to the right of F and draw a parallel line to AB. Label the points D and E respectively. Join up D to A and E to B.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish Band
steps.content: Add a 1.5cm seam allowance to all edges

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish Band
steps.content: Label ‘BAND, CUT TWO PAIRS’. Label the shorter edge ‘TOP’. Add a grainline perpendicular to the two long edges.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Make the Ties
steps.content: Take the top hip measurement and multiply by 1.7. Use this measurement to draw a horizontal line (line AB).

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Make the Ties
steps.content: From A, square down by 10cm (width of band minus 1cm), creating line AC.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Make the Ties
steps.content: From B, measure back 60cm along line AB creating point E. Square down 10cm from E, creating point D. Join C to D in a line parallel to line AB. Then from D, draw a gently curved line that meets point B.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Finish Ties
steps.content: Add 1.5cm seam allowance to all of the edges. Label ‘TIES, CUT TWO PAIRS’. Label line AB ‘TOP’.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Finish Ties
steps.content: The grainline should be perpendicular to the long edges. You can put the grainline parallel to the long edges but this will mean a directional print fabric would not work.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Make the Binding
steps.content: Measure the neckline on the bodice pattern without seam allowances and multiply by 4.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Make the Binding
steps.content: Cut a strip of fabric on the bias, measuring 3cm wide and as long as the neckline you just calculated, plus 2cm to allow for finishing the ends.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors and mark accurately to ensure clean, precise cuts.

steps.title: Add Band to Bodice
steps.content: Gather the bottom edge of the front and back bodices. Pull the gathers to half of your top hip measurement and tie a knot in the thread ends. Evenly distribute the gathers.

tip.text: Work carefully and check alignment to achieve a clean and accurate result.

steps.title: Add Band to Bodice
steps.content: Place the top edge of the band right sides together with the gathered edge of the bodice. Pin and stitch in place. Overlock or zigzag the seams and press up towards the bodice. Repeat with the back band and bodice.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Add the Ties
steps.content: Keeping one pair of ties right sides together, pin in place and stitch along the long edges, keeping the short, straight edge open. Trim down the seam allowance and cut off the point at the end. Turn right way round.

tip.text: Turn gently and support seams to prevent stress or tearing, especially around corners.

steps.title: Add the Ties
steps.content: Press flat, keeping the seams right on the edge. Tease the point using a pin. Repeat with the other pair of ties.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Add the Ties
steps.content: Pin the ties to the front band, aligning the raw edges. The top of the tie (straight edge) should sit right up to the seam with the bodice. This leaves 1cm of band extending out. Machine tack in place at 1cm.

tip.text: Always pin at key points to prevent fabric shifting during stitching.

steps.title: Join Bodices
steps.content: Take the back bodice and place the right sides together with the front. Pin the side seams together from the armhole notch H (or G for the exception) to the hem, sandwiching the ties. Align the seams and edges, pin in place and stitch. Overlock or zigzag the seams together and press towards the back

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Join Bodices
steps.content: Align the shoulder seams, right sides together. Pin in place and stitch. Overlock or zigzag the seams together and press towards the back.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Join Bodices
steps.content: At the armholes, working from the wrong side, press over 7mm and then a further 8mm clipping into the notch to allow the hem to sit flat. Edge stitch down.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Join Bodices
steps.content: Overlock or zigzag the bottom of the band. Press over the remaining seam allowance and stitch in place.

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Join Bodices
steps.content: Take the bias strip and join the ends, right sides together. Trim down the seam allowance and press open. Fold over 1cm along one edge of the bias strip. Press in place.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.

steps.title: Join Bodices
steps.content: Pin the unfolded edge of the bias strip right sides together with the neckline, lining up the seam on the bias with one of the shoulder seams. Stitch with a 1cm seam allowance. Trim down the seam allowance to 5mm. Press the seam allowance up towards the bias strip. Understitch along the bias strip

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Join Bodices
steps.content: Tuck the bias strip inside the neckline, rolling the top edge of the neckline so that you cannot see the bias strip. Press again and pin in place. Edge stitch the bias strip down to the neckline

tip.text: Maintain a consistent seam allowance and guide fabric evenly for a clean, professional finish.

steps.title: Join Bodices
steps.content: Give the top a final press.

tip.text: Press seams as you go to set stitches and improve overall garment quality.
